title: XRP Ledger Apex 2025
description: Multi-kiosk touchscreen installation for Ripple's global blockchain conference. Guided attendees through curated topics, collected preferences, and generated personalized personas. Guests received commemorative pins and follow-up emails with customized content.
technologies: TouchDesigner; touchscreen displays; kiosk systems
collaborators: Dave Tennent (Studio Dave Tennent); Trevor Grove (Invisible North); Sanaz and the CT team in Singapore

title: Camata
location: Marian Goodman Gallery NYC; Punta della Dogana, Venice
description: AI-operated film installation with autonomous cameras and robotic operators performing rituals on a human skeleton found in the Atacama Desert. Live sensors capture audience presence, which disrupts and edits the footage in real time. Built the backend for real-time editing and programmed custom sensors responding to human interaction.
technologies: TouchDesigner; IR sensors; real-time processing
collaborators: Anne-So Tisseyre; Valentin Giillet; Pablo Verdugo; Pedro Roca; Paola Ravagni; Pamela Valenzuela; Cadmos (AV installation); Ivaylo Getov; Tommy Martinez
notes: Existing page in repo at content/projects/camata.md

title: Crypto Sirenas
role: Technical director and projection systems designer; led installation team
description: Projection installation for a temporary collective exhibition featuring Berlin and San Salvador based artists. Designed a four-projector configuration for an 8.5-meter curved screen, optimizing for brightness, pixel density, and cost. Programmed a custom TouchDesigner control interface for playback and system management; projection mapping via MadMapper with NDI and SDI-HDMI conversion.
technologies: TouchDesigner; MadMapper; Vectorworks; Immersive Designer Pro; projection mapping; NDI; SDI-HDMI micro converters
collaborators: Zach Feldman (curator/producer); Chloe Munkenbeck (architect); Seth Scoville and Charlie Gangemi (install); Cam Vokey (pre-production); Max Levin; artists Anna Ehrenstein, Sunny Pfalzer, Lucy Tomasino, Alexa Evangelista; Josh Davila (web-3 researcher)

title: Kodansha House
client: Studio Dave Tennent / Kodansha House SoHo
description: Immersive pop-up installation inspired by manga classics Akira and Attack on Titan. Featured two timeline-based video booths replicating iconic moments from Kodansha-produced anime. Replicated compositions in TouchDesigner, developed two monitoring GUIs, and managed interactive development, installation, and project oversight.
technologies: TouchDesigner; Node-RED; FFMPEG; NDI; Brainbox
collaborators: Dave Tennent; Cam Vokey; Ashur Rayis; Susan Linns; Jeff Nash; Ghost Corporeal; Livesight; Hornet

title: Fantastic Flora
description: Immersive installation featuring a large suspended flower structure offering visitors a peaceful refuge during the festival. Functions as an acoustic experience: holding an ear to it produces tropical bird calls and rainforest sounds rather than ocean waves.
technologies: WLED; Arduino; lighting systems; audio integration
collaborators: Shreya Patel (design + fabrication); Andrew Giugno / Lowres; Nikki Cohen

title: Elementa @ The Gallery
description: Immersive dining experience at The Gallery restaurant featuring interactive visual installations for five elemental themes. Built TouchDesigner interactive systems using real-time hand and object tracking to create multiple responsive states across the dining experience.
technologies: TouchDesigner; YOLO tracking module; GLSL; real-time hand and object tracking
collaborators: Dave Tennent (Studio Dave Tennent); Luke Hildreth; Moustrappe Media creative and technical team; photographers Michael Juliano (Time Out), Wally Skalij (LA Times), Stage and Cinema

title: The Blue Paradox
client: Museum of Science and Industry, Chicago
description: Immersive, interactive, and educational exhibition about the global ocean plastic crisis. Developed through a major collaborative effort involving designers, programmers, and production crews.
technologies: TouchDesigner; After Effects; Arduino; Python; ScreenConnect; GitHub
collaborators: Sean Leo; Cam Vokey; Dave Tennent; Trevor Grove; Ben Bauer (photography); John Syzonenko (photography)
notes: Client conflict: homepage card credits Radical Media, detail page credits MSI Chicago. Verify before publishing.

title: Miro Canvas '24
description: Set up and managed a demo room for Miro's annual Canvas conference. A custom wraparound desk housed ten Mac Minis in kiosk mode so guests could access their Miro accounts without reaching the desktop. Four mounted projectors were blended and color-corrected in MadMapper with QLab managing playback.
technologies: Mac Mini kiosk mode; MadMapper; QLab; projection blending

title: ION Media Fan Connection Zone
location: Vanderbilt Hall, Grand Central Station, NYC
description: Immersive pop-up activation with an FBI-themed video wall carrying live surveillance feeds at the entrance, paired with an interactive soccer skills booth for the NWSL. Guests selected a favorite team via a browser interface; scoring triggered celebration animations driven by 2D LiDAR sensors.
technologies: TouchDesigner; Node-RED; NDI; 2D LiDAR; video wall systems
collaborators: Charlie Gangemi; Lake Heckaman (LiDAR); MPC; Pablo Gnecco; Civic Entertainment (producer); Chemistry Creative (producer)

title: INTER_
location: Historic National City Bank of New York, SoHo/Chinatown, NYC
description: Two-story immersive interactive venue with dozens of projectors, 4K displays, LED walls, and hundreds of feet of video and data cabling. Visitors pass through rooms with interactive moments using live-camera feedback; those interactions are temporarily recorded and used to generate art displayed in the final room. Includes an orientation film room, mirrored escalator with embedded displays and cameras, 360-degree projection space with LiDAR tracking, psychedelic garden with Arduino-controlled lights, a Cymatic Well with crystal ball controllers, an interactive cavern, and the Interconnected gallery.
technologies: TouchDesigner; After Effects; Blender; Unreal Engine; Arduino; LiDAR; Python; NDI cameras; Azure sensors; Leap Motion; Ableton Live
collaborators: Dave Tennent; Jonathan Masterson; Georgios Cherouvim; Beau Burrows; Tommy Martinez; Taliesin Gilkes-Bower; Oliver Chapoy; Danny Taylor; MPC; 30+ credited scenic and fabrication specialists
notes: Full 30+ credit list not captured; re-scrape detail page if you want complete credits.

title: Nike Air SoHo
client: Nike (Air Max campaign) via Chemistry Creative
description: Large-scale interactive multimedia installation built on TouchDesigner. Idle state shows the Nike logo with looping cloud animations; on detecting visitor presence the clouds shift color and inspirational quotes appear. Had a brief public lifespan before Covid-19 closures.
technologies: TouchDesigner; After Effects; Blender; RealSense; LED stage
collaborators: Chemistry Creative; Lovejoy Paradise (supervisor); Vincent Houze (TouchDesigner/sensor placement)

title: Ledger OP3N
description: Interactive installation for NFT Week 2022. The centerpiece was an orange acrylic cube containing a suspended Ledger Nano that, when touched, triggered synchronized animations across screens throughout the venue. Combined idle and takeover animations with a 24-screen mosaic and an LED wall.
technologies: TouchDesigner; After Effects; Arduino; pressure sensors
collaborators: Charlie Gangemi; Chemistry Creative design/install team; TXG; Jam3

title: Diesel Sound of the Brave
description: Fragrance launch event with stage design built around 2 LED walls and 8 large flanking displays. DJ and performer sets ran alongside Diesel fragrance content, plus room activations including an LED wall photo booth with a variety of backgrounds.
technologies: TouchDesigner; After Effects; LED stage systems; pressure sensors
collaborators: Chemistry Creative; Pete Sax (stage design); Greg Zifcak (V1 operations); Jason Crowley (photo, BFA/TXG)

title: Laser Control
description: Music festival project developing looping laser animations for the main camping area, built with a combination of After Effects and MadMapper and displayed throughout the weekend event.
technologies: After Effects; MadMapper; lasers
notes: Index lists as Shaker Mountain 2023; homepage/nav label is Laser Control.

title: Thought Patterns
description: Live music performance and interview series with audio-reactive generative visuals. Orchestrated and captured over a dozen live performance videos, created dynamic visuals projected on a large LED stage, ran real-time control of display surfaces, and handled weekly editing and publishing. Also covered virtual production and sound design for the short film Tether Tear. Emerged from pandemic isolation as a means of connecting through music.
technologies: TouchDesigner; After Effects; Premiere Pro; LED stage; live camera feedback
collaborators: Pete Sax; Nicolas Motyka; Sydney MacDonald; Christopher Robinson; Danny Taylor; Charlie Gangemi; Monty; Danny Ballester; Kevin Condor
notes: Also the home for the Endless Morning music video.

title: Is it Okay? NFT
description: Digital art / NFT piece.
notes: Index links out to foundation.app; no detail page on site.

title: Spiegel im Spiegel
description: Custom visuals accompanying live performances of Arvo Part's composition. Design began with imagery from the Hubble and James Webb telescopes, emphasizing connections between macro and micro phenomena; later iterations incorporated photographer Austin Mann's work for region-specific visuals. Sequence has been customized for performances across multiple continents. Responsibilities covered image research, arrangement, experimentation, timeline editing, and final delivery.
technologies: Premiere Pro; After Effects
collaborators: Sophie Shakleton (creative producer); Zion Jackson and THE OFFICE; Austin Mann (photographer); photo credit UN Partnerships/Pier Paolo Cito

title: Incarceration Nations: A Global Docuseries
description: Mixed-media educational series examining global mass incarceration across ten episodes, narrated by incarcerated and formerly incarcerated individuals from eight countries. Synthesizes interview footage and B-roll collected over years of prison visits, presenting both the crisis and solutions through work by justice partners worldwide. Selected by Tribeca Film Festival in 2021; screened in prisons, classrooms, museums, and community centers globally.
technologies: Premiere Pro; After Effects; TouchDesigner; Ableton; AnimVR; Procreate; Photoshop
collaborators: Dr. Baz Dreisinger (director); Sydney MacDonald (coordination); Matthew Wilson (illustrations); Nicolas Motyka (assembly/editing); Danny Taylor (audio)
notes: Image filenames returned for this page were Blue Paradox assets - scraper bleed, do not trust asset list.
